questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select the option that contains all plants which produce non-endospermic seeds.
questions.solution: Non-endospermic seeds or exalbuminous seeds have no residual endosperm as it is completely consumed during embryo development. Gram, pea, bean and groundnut have non-endospermic seeds.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Composite fruit develops from
questions.solution: Composite fruits are multiple fruits which are developed from the complete inflorescence and are also called as infructescence. They are of two types sorosis and syconus. Spike, spadix and catkin type of inflorescence gives rise to this type of fruit eg Ananas, Artocarpus. Syconus develops from a hypanthodium inflorescence eg Ficus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The family containing mustard and its main characters are
options: Brassicaceae - Tetramerous flowers, six stamens, bicarpellary gynoecium, siliqua type fruit
Brassicaceae - Pentamerous flowers, many stamens, pentacarpellary gynoecium, capsule type fruit
Solanaceae - Pentamerous flowers, five stamens, bicarpellary gynoecium, berry type fruit
Poaceae - Trimerous flowers, three stamens, monocarpellary gynoecium, caryopsis type of fruit.
questions.solution: Brassica (mustard) belongs to family Brassicaceae. Family brassicaceae is characterised by polypetalous 4 petals, polysepalous 4 sepals, six stamens (which are in tetradynamous condition i.e. 4 +2 or in two whorls, 4 inner long and 2 outer short). Gynoecium is bicarpellary, syncarpous unilocular with parietal placentation. Fruit is usually siliqua (dry dehiscent fruit formed by bicarpellary syncarpous gynoecium with replum) and rarely lomentum.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Cocoa is the plant from which chocolate is made. Which part is used to extract it?
questions.solution: Cocoa or Theobroma belongs to family sterculiaceae, and is a native of tropical America for preparation of cocoa and chocolates, the seeds are used.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In which one of the following would you expect to find glyoxysomes?
questions.solution: Glyoxysomes are enzymes which play a critical role in lipid metabolism in seedlings. Hence they are supposed to be present in endosperm of castor as endosperm of wheat will posses starch.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: When the filament runs throughout the length of anther, it is called
questions.solution: In adnate type filament attachment, the filament runs throughout the length of anther. It is found in Ranunculus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which floral family has (9) +1 arrangement of anthers in the androecium ?
questions.solution: In Fabaceae stamens have diadelphous arrangement i.e. (9) + 1. In total these are 10 in number.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Clove is a
questions.solution: Cloves are unopened floral buds of Syzygium aromaticum.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Coconut's husk fibre, coir of commerce is extracted from
questions.solution: Commercial coir is obtained from the fibrous husk (mesocarp) of fruits of coconut palm. The fibre is valued for its lightness and exceedingly high resistance to mechanical wear and dampness.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: During formation of which of the following free nuclear division occurs?
questions.solution: In nuclear endosperm the primary endosperm nucleus divides repeatedly without wall formation producing a large number of free nuclei. It is most common type of endosperm.

questions.solution: No Solution Available

A : scutellum B : pericarp $\mathrm{C}$ : radicle $\mathrm{D}$ : coleoptile
A : endosperm B : scutellum C : radicle D: coleorrhiza

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : The two cotyledons in the seed are often referred to as the embryonic leaves.
Reason : The embryo contains radicle and plumule.
options: If both the assertion and reason are true and reason is a correct explanation of the assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but assertion is not a correct explanation of the assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: In a typical angiospermic plant, cotyledons in the seed are the embryonic leaves. The radicle of the embryo upon the seed germination produces the root system and the plumule produces the shoot system. The radicle and plumule are contained in the embryo.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Tetradynamous condition is found in
questions.solution: In tetradynamous condition there is $4+2$ arrangement of stamens i.e., out of six stamens, 4 are long and 2 are short. It is a characterstic feature of members of family Brassicaceae.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Read the given statements and identify the PGRs $X, Y$ and $Z$.
$\mathrm{X}$ induces accumulation of salts inside the cells.
Y promotes rapid elongation of leaf bases and internodes in deep water rice plants.
(iii) Application of $Z$ to leaves shall reduce transpiration.
questions.solution: Cytokinins induce accumulation of salts inside the cells. Ethylene promotes rapid elongation of leaf bases and internodes in deep water rice plants. As a result, leaves remain above water. Application of minute quantity of abscisic acid to leaves shall reduce transpiration to a great extent through partial closure of stomata. It conserves water and reduces the requirement of irrigation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Identify the incorrect statement about $\mathrm{ABA}$ growth regulator.
options: It increases the tolerance of plants against different stresses.
It acts as general plant growth inhibitor and inhibitor of metabolism.
It helps in seed maturation and dormancy.
It promotes morphogenesis and differentiation of shoots.
questions.solution: Abscisic acid (ABA) is a growth inhibitor. Both auxins and cytokinins are essential for morphogenesis or differentiation of tissues and organs. Auxins stimulate root formation however when cytokinins are in excess stem buds develop.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Gibberellins can promote seed germination because of their influence on
options: absorption of water through hard seed coat.
questions.solution: Gibberellins induce the production of hydrolysing enzymes in the endosperm of germinating seeds. It stimulates the production of digestive enzymes like proteases, amylase and lipase which help to mobilise stored nutrients.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which hormone inhibits morphogenesis?
questions.solution: Abscisic acid is a mildly acidic dextrorotatory cis sesquiterpene growth hormone which functions as a general growth inhibitor by counteracting other hormones (auxin, gibberellins, cytokinins) or reactions mediated by them. Abscisic acid is known as dormin as it induces dormancy in buds, underground stems and seeds.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following sets does not include all natural plant growth regulators?
questions.solution: Plant growth regulators are small, simple molecules of diverse chemical composition, which in low concentration regulate growth, differentiation and development by promoting or inhibiting the same. Kinetin is a synthetic cytokinin and zinc $(\mathrm{Zn})$ is not a natural plant growth regulator.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is not a bioassay for cytokinins?
questions.solution: Cytokinins have the ability to retard the degradation of chlorophyll in excised leaves. Hence, chlorophyll preservation test is a bioassay for cytokinin. Cytokinin promotes cell enlargement in the leaf tissues of certain plants. So, radish and bean leaf test have been used as bioassay. Promotion of lettuce seed germination forms the basis for another bioassay for cytokinins. Dwarf maize test is a bioassay for gibberellins as they induce internodal growth in genetically dwarf varieties of plants like pea and maize.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which among the following are auxins?
questions.solution: IAA is Indole 3 acetic acid is universal natural auxin. IBA is indole 3 butyric acid with auxin like activity. It is both naturally occurring and synthetically prepared. NAA is napthalene acetic acid which is a synthetic auxin.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : A correct concentration of auxin and cytokinin is required for the development of root and shoot in a callus.
Reason : When the ratio of kinetin to auxin is high, then only shoots develop. But when the ratio is low then only roots develop.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
Ifboth assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: The relative ratio of cytokinin (kinetin) to auxin (IAA) is very important in determining the nature of organogenesis in tissue culture. High level of kinetin results in bud initiation, while high concentration of auxin favours rooting.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Plant growth regulators (PGRs) are very important for plant growth and development.
Reason : Auxins do not induce flowering in gymnosperms.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
Ifboth assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Plant growth regulators (PGRs) are small, simple molecules of diverse chemical composition, which in low concentration regulate growth, differentiation and development by promoting or inhibiting the same. One type of plant growth regulators are plant hormones or phytohormones. Auxins are the phytohormones. They do not induce flowering in gymnosperms.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following plant growth regulators (PGRs) promotes root initiation, flowering and induced parthenocarpy?
questions.solution: Auxins promote root initiation at a concentration which otherwise is inhibitory for growth of intact root. Auxins are often employed for inducing flowering in litchi and pineapple. Application of auxins to unpollinated pistils make them develop into seedless fruits or parthenocarps.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : On plotting the length of the root against time, a linear curve is obtained. 
Reason : An elongating root exemplifies arithmetic growth.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false
questions.solution: In arithmetic growth, following mitotic cell division, only one daughter cell continues to divide while the other differentiates and matures. The simplest expression of arithmetic growth is exemplified by a root elongating at a constant rate. On plotting the length of the organ against time, a linear curve is obtained.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Bacteria, fungi, lower plants survive in adverse conditions by
questions.solution: Bacteria, fungi and lower plants survive in adverse conditions through formation of thick walled spores. Algae form resting spore or akinetes which are thick walled spores and store food material. In fungi, thick walled perennating spores called chlamydospores are formed. Bacteria form endospores which is probably the most resistant living thing known. They may remain alive in boiling water for more than 2 hours.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Vernalisation is subjected to plants growing in
questions.solution: Vernalisation refers specially to the promotion of flowering by exposure to a period of low temperature. Many temperate plants have vernalisation requirement and must experience a period of low temperature in winter, to initiate or accelerate flowering process, or in many fruit tree species, to induce dormancy and then break dormancy prior to flowering.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
What are labelled phases $A, B$ and $C$ in given sigmoidal growth curve?
$$ \text { A } \quad \text { B } \quad \text { C } $$
questions.solution:
When a graph is plotted taking into consideration time on one hand and growth rate on the other hand, a 'S' shaped curve is obtained. It is called "grand period of growth". This total growth period is divided into three stages.
(1) Initial lag phase
(2) Middle log phase
(3) Final stationary phase.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Plant growth as a whole is indefinite. 
Reason : Plants retain the capacity of continuous growth throughout their life.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Unlike animals, plants do not stop growing after reaching maturity. They continue to grow and bear new roots, leaves, branches, flowers, etc. While roots, stems and their branches have indefinite growth, other organs like leaves, flowers and fruits show limited or definite growth. They appear and fall off periodically and sometimes repeatedly. In lower plants, growth is diffused as every cell can divide and enlarge. Higher plants possess specific areas which take part in the formation of new cells. These areas are called meristems. Meristems are of three types- apical, intercalary and lateral. Cells of the meristematic region have the capacity to divide and self perpetuate. They produce cells which lose the capacity to divide and enter $G_0$ phase for undergoing differentiation to form particular tissues and organs. The meristem which is consumed in the formation of an organ is called determinate meristem. The meristem which continues its activity throughout life of the plant is called indeterminate meristem. It is the reason behind indefinite growth of plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Cytokinins increases shelf life of fruits and vegetables.
Reason : Cytokinins induce cell division.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Application of cytokinins to marketed vegetables can keep them fresh for several days. Shelf life of cut shoots and flowers is prolonged by employing the hormone cytokinin. The reason is that the cytokinin increases retention of chlorophyll and delayed senescence in leaves. Cytokinin as the name suggests, is essential for cell division or cytokinesis. It promotes cell division along with auxin by controlling the activity of cyclin dependent kinases.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Avena curvature test is a bioassay for examining the activity of
questions.solution: Bioassay is a testing of biological activity like growth response of a substance by employing plant part. Avena curvature test is a bioassay for examining the activity of auxins. It is based upon experiments of Went. $10^{\circ}$ curvature is produced by auxin concentration of $150 \mu \mathrm{g} /$ lit at $25^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and $90 \%$ relative humidity. It can measure auxin upto 300 $\mu \mathrm{g}$ /itre. Auxin from shoot tip or any other plant organ is allowed to diffuse in a standard size agar block. 15 - $30 \mathrm{~mm}$ long oat coleoptile grown in dark is held vertically over water. $1 \mathrm{~mm}$ tip of coleoptile is removed without injuring the primary leaf. After 3 hours a second decapitation is carried out for $4 \mathrm{~mm}$ distance. Primary leaf is now pulled loose and agar block supported against it at the tip of decapitated coleoptile. After $90-110 \mathrm{~mm}$ the coleoptile is found to have bent. The curvature is measured.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In an experiment freshly hatched larvae of an insect (Khapra beetle) were reared on a basal diet (complete diet without cholesterol) with increasing amounts of cholesterol. Results obtained are shown in the graph given in the table : The graph indicates that
options: cholesterol is an essential dietary requirement of khapra beetle
growth of khapra beetle is directly proportional to cholesterol concentration
cholesterol concentration of $2 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{g}$ diet is the optimum level
growth of khapra beetle is inhibited when cholesterol concentration exceeds $5 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{g}$ diet.
questions.solution: According to the graph the growth of the insect takes place as the amount of the cholesterol increase and at $6 \mu \mathrm{g}$ cholesterol $/ \mathrm{g}$ the growth get static. If the growth rate would have been directly proportional to the cholesterol concentration then the graph would have been straight line. (3) is wrong as the optimum level is $6 \mu \mathrm{g} / \mathrm{g}$ cholesterol concentration.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Gibberellins can promote seed germination because of their influence on
options: absorption of water through hard seed coat.
questions.solution: Gibberellins induces the hydrolyzing enzymes in the endosperm of germinating seeds. It stimulates the production of digestive enzymes like proteases, amylase, lipase which help to mobilise stored nutrients.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Senescence is the time when age associated defects are manifested.
Reason : Certain genes may be undergoing sequential switching on and off during one's life.
options: If both Assertion and Reason are true and the Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion
If both Assertion and Reason are true but the Reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion
If Assertion is true but Reason is false.
If both Assertion and Reason are false.
questions.solution: Process of ageing is called senescence. Ageing, is cumulative changes in an organism, organ, tissue, or cell leading to a decrease in functional capacity. In humans, aging is associated with degenerative changes in the skin, bones, heart, blood vessels, lungs, nerves, and other organs and tissues. It is genetically controlled programme regulated by regulatory genes. This view is suggested by the great range of lifespans among different animal speciesfrom a few days in the fruit fly to more than 100 years in some tortoises. Scientists have recently learned how to double the lifespans of such laboratory organisms as roundworms and fruit flies through genetic manipulation, and mutant genes in mice have been observed to have a comparable effect in postponing ageing.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: One hormone hastens maturity period in juvenile conifers, a second hormone controls xylem differentiation, while the third hormone increases the tolerance of plants to various stresses. They are respectively
questions.solution: Spraying juvenile conifers with gibberellins hastens their maturity period, thus leading to early seed production. Auxin controls xylem differentiation. Abscisic acid (ABA) increases the tolerance of plants to various kinds of stresses therefore, is also known as stress hormone.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Phytohormones are
options: hormones regulating growth from seed to adulthood
growth regulators synthesised by plants and influencing physiological process
questions.solution: Growth regulators are organic substances, other than nutrients, which in low concentration regulate growth, differentiation and development by promoting or inhibiting the same. Phytohormones are growth regulators produced naturally in plants and translocated to another region for regulating one or more physiological reactions when present in low concentration. Phytohormone can have a promoting or inhibiting effect on a process.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Apomixis is
options: formation of seeds by fusion of gametes
formation of seeds without syngamy and meiosis
formation of seeds with syngamy but no meiosis
questions.solution: Apomixis (Gk. apo-without, mixismixing) is a mode of reproduction which does not involve formation of zygote through gametic fusion. It is, therefore, akin to asexual reproduction. In plants apomixis commonly mimics sexual reproduction but produces seeds without fertilisation, e.g., some species of Asteraceae and grasses.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : In vernalisation, flowering is induced by low temperature.
Reason : ABA is growth inhibiting hormone.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Gibberellic acid increases length of grape stalks.
Reason : Ethophon hastens fruit ripening in tomato and apple.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Gibberellin increases the number and size of several fruits, e.g., grape, tomato. The hormone creates more room by increasing the size of stalks so that the fruit can grow in size. Ethophon is 2-chloroethyl phosphonic acid. In aqueous solution, ethophon slowly releases ethylene and is transported to various parts. It aids in fruit ripening in some fleshy fruits like banana, tomato, apple, mango, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Storage of seeds at low temperature is possible.
Reason : Respiration and enzymatic activity of seeds are very high at low temperature.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Photomodulation of flowering is a phytochrome - regulated process.
Reason : Active form of phytochrome (Pfr) directly induces floral induction in shoot buds.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Flowering is a phytochrome regulated process. Phytotchrome is a blue protein pigment with a molecular mass of about $125 \mathrm{k} \mathrm{Da}$ (kilodaltons). Phytochrome occurs in two interconvertible form $P_R$ (which absorbs red light) and $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{fr}}$ (which absorbs far red light). The studies with mutants of Arabidopsis support and prove that the $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{fr}}$ is the active form of phytochrome. When $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{R}}$ molecules are exposed to red light, most of them absorb it and converted to $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{fr}}$. This causes floral induction in the shoot.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Bud dormancy is induced by
questions.solution: The bud dormancy is induced by adantscissic acid (ABA). The level of endogenous ABA increases with the onset of dormant period and decreases when it is broken.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Death is regarded as the most important regulatory process on earth.
Reason : It avoids overcrowding, caused by continuous reproduction.
options: If both the assertion and reason are true and reason is a correct explanation of the assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but assertion is not a correct explanation of the assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Death is regarded as the most important regulatory process on earth. It reduces the number of individuals in a population and avoids overcrowding caused by continuous reproduction. Overcrowding due to reproduction is countered by death as the ultimate termination of functional life.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following pesticides is an acetylcho linesterase inhibitor?
questions.solution: Organophosphate (Malathion) kills insects by inhibiting enzyme cholinesterase. It is organic ester of carbonic acid. In structure, it is quite similar to acetylcholine and, therefore has strong affinity for the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, and inhibits its activity.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following statements is correct regarding short day plants (SDPs)?
options: They can flower in complete darkness if supplied with exogenous nutrients.
They are the same as long day plants.
They do not have a critical photoperiod.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Abscisic acid is known as the stress hormone because it
questions.solution: Abscisic acid acts as a general plant growth inhibitor and an inhibitor of plant metabolism. ABA inhibits seed germination. ABA stimulates the closure of stomata in the epidermis and increases the tolerance of plants to various kinds of stresses. Therefore, it is also called the stress hormone.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Refer the given figures on photoperiodism and select the correct option.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Vernalization is acceleration of subsquent flowering by low temperature treatment.
Reason : Site of vernalization is apical meristem.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Term vernalization was first given by T.D. Lysenko (1928). In colder countries, there are cereals of 2 physiological types, i.e., winter cereals and spring cereals. Winter cereals are sown in winter and flower in following summer whereas spring cereals are sown in spring and flower in summer. If winter cereal is sown in spring, it shows vegetative growth but does not flower unless it receives low temperature of winter. But winter cereal can be converted into spring cereal by providing artificial low temperature treatment, (i.e., springification.) Some plants requiring low temperature requirement (vernalization) for flowering are Secale cereale (European rye), Triticum vulgare, Brassica oleracea, Beta vulgaris, Apium and Lolium perenne, etc. Site of vernalization is apical meristem or all the meristematic cells. As a result of vernalization, a flowering hormone called "vernalin" is formed (reported by Melchers), but venalin has never been isolated. Once a plant is vernalized, it can be devernalized by exposing the plant to temperature of \(30^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) or above. For establishing vernalization, plant should be kept at \(20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\) for \(4-5\) days.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pruning of plants promotes branching because the axillary buds get sensitized to
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Induction of flowering by low temperature in plants is
questions.solution: Vernalization is a process of shortening of the juvenile or vegetative phase and hastening flowering by a previous cold treatment. The term vernalization was coined by Lysenko (1928).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.
options: Absence of resin duct and leaf venation
Presence of vessel elements and absence of archegonia
questions.solution: Unlike Cycas and Pinus, Gnetum shows the occurrence of vessel elements and the complete absence of archegonia in female gametophyte. Thus, Gnetum shows affinities with angiosperms. Besides its resembles with angiosperms in several other aspects like presence of tetrasporic embryo sac, free nuclear divisions in the embryo sac, two cotyledonous embryo, etc.

Reason : Peat helps to keep soil porous and it also improves water holding capacity of the soil.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Petroplants produce large amount of latex.
Reason: The latex contains long chain hydrocarbons.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Latex of some plants containing long chain hydrocarbons is considered to be a good substitute for liquid fuels or petroleum. Such plants having large amount of latex with long chain hydrocarbons are called petroplants. Cultivation of petroplants is also a part of energy-cropping. The most common petroplants belong to families of euphorbiaceae, asclepiadaceae, apocynaceae, compositae, labiatae, sapotaceae, urticaceae, etc., which have property of converting large amounts of their photosynthates into latex with hydrocarbons. The use of these petroplants may reduce the pressure on liquid fuel or petroleum.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Pick the correct group consisting of all genera exhibiting homospory.
questions.solution: Pteridophytes, may produce similar spores (homosporous) or dissimilar spores (heterosporous). Selaginella and Salvinia show heterospory.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.
options: Absence of resin duct and leaf venation.
Presence of vessel elements and absence of archegonia.
questions.solution: Unlike Cycas and Pinus, Gnetum shows the occurrence of vessel elements and the absence of archegonia. Thus, Gnetum shows affinities with angiosperms. Besides,it resembles angiosperms in several other aspects like presence of tetrasporic embryo sac, free nuclear divisions in the embryo sac, two cotyledonous embryo, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Which of the following are homosporous pteridophytes?
I. Selaginella
II. Lycopodium
III. Salvinia
IV. Equisetum
questions.solution: Genera like Selaginella and Salvinia which produce two kinds of spores, macro (large) and micro (small) spores, are known as heterosporous pteridophytes Lycopodium and Equisetum are homosporous pteridophytes. i.e. they produce only one type of spores.

Assertion : Heterospory and retention of female gametophyte are responsible for origin of seed habit in Selaginella sp.
Reason : Psilotum $\mathrm{sp}$. can be regarded as a living fossil.

If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is the correct sequence of the formation of female gametophyte in angiosperms?
options: Nucellus, megapore tetrad, megaspore mother cell, megaspore, female gametophyte
Megaspore tetrad, nucellus, megaspore mother cell, megaspore, female gametophyte
Nucellus, megaspore mother cell, megaspore tetrad, megaspore, female gametophyte
Megaspore mother cell, megaspore tetrad, megaspore, nucellus, female gametophyte
questions.solution: In angiosperms, the body of the ovule consists of a mass of parenchymatous cells called nucellus. In the hypodermal region of nucellus towards the micropylar end develops a primary archesporial cell, which by further divisions forms a diploid megaspore mother cell. The latter undergoes meiosis to form megaspore tetrad. Usually the chalazal megaspore remains functional while the other three degenerate. The functional megaspore is the first cell of female gametophyte (embryo sac).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the given genera is homosporous?
questions.solution: In pteridophytes, majority of plants are homosporous (producing similar kind of spores) e.g., Lycopodium. However, plants of genera like Selaginella and Salvinia are heterosporous (producing macro and micro spores). Pinus and Cycas, which are gymnosperms, are also heterosporous.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following groups of algae produces algin?
questions.solution: Alginic acid (or algin or alginate) is an anionic polysaccharide distributed widely in the cell walls of many brown algae (Phaeophyceae). It is a phycocolloid which is obtained commercially from Laminaria, Macrocystis, Nereocystis, Fucus, Sargassum etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : The leaves in gymosperms are welladapted to withstand extremes of temperature, humidity and wind.
Reason: Unlike bryophytes and pteridophytes, in gymnosperms the male and female gametophytes do not have an independent free-living existence.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: The leaves in gymnosperms are welladapted to withstand extremes of temperature, humidity and wind. In conifers, the needlelike leaves reduce the surface area. Their thick cuticle and sunken stomata also help to reduce water loss. Unlike bryophytes and pteridophytes, in gymnosperms the male and the female gametophytes do not have an independent free-living existence. They remain within the sporangia retained on the sporophytes.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which statement is correct regarding mosses ?
options: They have dominant and independent sporophyte.
Their antherozoids require water for fertilization.
questions.solution: In mosses the sporophytes (i.e. the diploid body) are short-lived and dependent on the gametophyte. In the presence of water, sperms from the antheridia swim to the archegonia and fertilization occurs, leading to the production of a diploid sporophyte. The sperm of mosses is biflagellate, i.e. they have two flagella that aid in propulsion. Since the sperms must swim to the archegonium, fertilization cannot occur without water.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Match Column - I with Column - II and select the correct option from the codes given below
options: $A-(i v), B-(i), C-(i i), D-(i i i)$
$A-(i i), B-(i i i), C-(i), D-(i v)$
$A-(i v), B-(i), C-(i i i), D-(i i)$

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : In angiosperms, transport of food and water is more efficient than gymnosperms and pteridophytes.
Reason : In angiosperms longitudinally arranged sieve elements and vessels with perforated end walls are present.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution:
Vessels are much elongated tubes which are closed at either end and are formed by the union of several short, wide and thickened cells called vessel elements. The end walls of vessel elements are transverse or oblique. They are often completely dissolved. The condition is called simple perforation plate. In a few cases the end walls remain intact and possess several pores in reticulate, scalariform or forminate forms. Such an end wall is called multiple perforation plate. Sieve tubes are elongated tubular conducting channels of phloem formed of several cells called sieve tube elements or members or sieve tube cells. Sieve tube members are placed end to end. The end walls are generally bulged out. They may be transverse or oblique. They have many small pores or sieve pits. Due to the presence of sieve pits the end walls are commonly called sieve plates. In some cases the end walls of sieve elements possess more than one porous area. Such an end wall is called compound sieve plate.
Gymnosperms \& pteridophytes lack these two vascular structures i.e. vessels and sieve elements. In angiosperms presence of vessels \& sieve elements in addition to all other vascular tissues make their food \& water transport more efficient.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Gametophyte and sporophyte are independent of each other in which of the following groups?
questions.solution: Bryophytes are nonvascular terrestrial plants of moist habitats in which a multicellular diploid sporophyte lives as a parasite on an independent multicellular haploid gametophyte that develops multicellular jacketed sex organs. Whereas, in pteridophytes, the main plant body is a sporophyte which is differentiated into true root, stem and leaves and gametophyte is small or inconspicuous, it is usually independent.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Algae and fungi are classified as thallophytes.
Reason : They both are autotrophs.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: According to Eichler's classification, plant kingdom is divided into two subdivisions cryptogamae and phanerogamae. Cryptogamae has 3 divisions thallophyta, bryophyta and pteridophyta. Thallophyta in turn is divided into 2 classes, algae and fungi. As both these groups have generally undifferentiated plant body (not differentiated into true root, stem and leaves) they are called thallophyta. Also they are non vascular plants. But algae are chlorophyllous and hence show autotrophic mode of nutrition ie can prepare their own food. But fungi are nonchlorophyllous and cannot prepare their own food. So they are heterotrophic in nature.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : The primary protenema of moss by death and decay of cells may break into fragments.
Reason : Each fragment gives rise to leafy gametophyte.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: The haploid spores on germination gives rise to alga-like filamentous branched portion called primary protonema. This protonema develops buds, from which leafy gametophyte arises. Sometimes this primary protonema breaks up into small fragments accidentally and these fragments give rise to leafy gametophores.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is the largest root parasite?
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Conifer trees produce a large quantity of wind borne pollen grains.
Reason : The pollen grains have wings.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Conifer or Pinus is a heterosporous plant producing mega and microspores inside megasporangium and micro-sporangium respectively. Each microsporangium is a small, sessile and elongated sac like structure having two layered wall. Inside the wall lie tapetum which is a nourishing layer. Inside the cavity are large number of dusty and two winged microspores present. On maturity the microsporangium wall bursts from the upper side forming a longitudinal slit. A large number of yellow pollen grains are liberated which are carried by wind due to the presence of wings.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Red algae contribute in producing coral reefs.
Reason : Some red algae secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution:
In some species of red algae called coralline algae, the cell walls become hardened with calcium carbonate. These algae hence important for the formation of coral reefs.
Coral reefs are formed through the accumulation of calcareous exoskeletons of coral animals, calcareous red algae and molluses. They form the foundation of reefs by secreting a calcium carbonate skeleton that provides protection for the coral polyps. Calcium carbonate is secreted continuously by the coral colony.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant part which consists of two generations, one within the other, is
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Algae are useful because they
options: are used in curdling of milk.
questions.solution: Maximum solar energy is trapped by algae and hence they evolve maximum amount of oxygen by the process of photosynthesis. Therefore, algae are useful because they purify the atmosphere by releasing oxygen.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select the incorrect statement.
options: Plants undergoing nonrecurrent agamospermy generally produce non-viable seeds.
In recurrent agamospermy all the cells of embryo sac are diploid as it is directly formed from nucellar or megaspore mother cell.
In gymnosperms, polyembryony may occur due to cleavage of growing embryo.
Polyembryony never occurs in mango and groundnut.
questions.solution: Polyembrony is quite common in onion, groundnut, mango, lemon, orange, etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The plant body of moss (Funaria) is
questions.solution: The gametophytic generation represents the dominant phase in the life cycle of bryophytes. The sporophyte phase is dependent on the gametophyte. That is why, the plant body of Funaria moss is predominantly gametophytic with dependent sporophyte.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following genera is associated with coralloid roots?
questions.solution: Roots in Cycas are of two types - normal and coralloid roots. Coralloid roots are irregular, negatively geotropic, dichotomously branched coral like roots which do not possess root hair and root caps. Coralloid roots have a symbiotic association with blue-green algae like Nostoc and Anabaena species.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following are homosporous?
questions.solution: The plants bearing similar spores are called homosporous, e.g., Pteris, Lycopodium, Equisetum, Adiantum, etc. Selaginella and Salvinia are heterosporous, i.e., they bear two types of spores - microspores and megaspores.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Elater mechanism for seed dispersal is exhibited by
questions.solution: Marchantia is a liverwort. Its sporophyte contains elongated cells with spiral thickenings on its wall called elaters. These elaters are hygroscopic in nature. The resulting twisting movement helps in dispersal of spores.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select one of the following pairs of important features distinguishing Gnetum from Cycas and Pinus and showing affinities with angiosperms.
options: Absence of resin duct and leaf venation
Presence of vessel elements and absence of archegonia
questions.solution: Unlike Cycas and Pinus, Gnetum shows the occurrence of vessel elements and the absence of archegonia. Archegonia are altogether absent in the female gametophyte and vessels occur in the xylem along with the tracheids in the secondary wood. Thus Gnetum shows affinities with angiosperms. Besides it resembles angiosperms in several other aspects like presence of tetrasporic embryo sac, free nuclear divisions in the embryo sac, two cotyledonous embryo etc.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which among the following is a rootless plant?
questions.solution: All the given options are hydrophytes in nature which grow in extremely wet or watery conditions. But among them only in Ceratophyllum roots are completely absent even in embryonic stage. They remain under water completely. Nymphaea is a rooted hydrophyte with floating leaves, Vallisneria is rooted submerged hydrophyte and Sagittaria is rooted emergent hydrophyte.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Mossess and ferns are found in moist and shady places because both
options: require presence of water for fertilization
do not need sunlight for photosynthesis
depend for their nutrition on microorganisms which can survive only at low temperature
questions.solution: During fertilization ventral canal cell and the neck canal cell, along with tip of the neck disintegrate and the neck forms a canal like passage full of mucilage and some chemicals like malic acids. These chemicals attract antherozoids (chemotactic movement of antherozoids) and one of the male gametes fuses with the oospore forming a diploid oospore. Water is essential for this process.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Red algae contribute in producing coral reefs.
Reason: Some red algae secrete and deposit calcium carbonate over their walls.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Coral reefs are formed through the accumulation of calcareous exoskeletons of coral animals, calcareous red algae and molluscs. They form the foundation of reefs by secreting a calcium carbonate skeleton that provides protection for the coral polyps.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: ATP formation occurs through which of the following processes?
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is correctly matched without exception in regard to plant classification?
questions.solution: All plant families end with -ae suffix. However, it differs for division, class and genus.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Among the primates, chimpanzee is the closest relative of the present day humans.
Reason : The banding pattern in the autosome numbers 3 and 6 of man and chimpanzee is remarkably similar.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution:
Carolus Linnaeus called humans as Homo sapiens or wise men and placed them along with apes and monkeys. There are certain similarities between human and chimpanzee. These are -
(i) RNA content of diploid cells is similar
(ii) DNA matching shows that human similarity is 100 $\%$ with chimpanzee and
(iii) banding pattern of chromosomes shows very little difference in chromosomes 3 and 6 between humans and chimpanzee. Thus it can be concluded that among the primates, chimpanzee is the closest relative of the present day humans.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Match the following columns and select the correct option.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The taxon which includes related species is
questions.solution: Genus comprises a group of related species which has more characters in common in comparison to species of other genera. In the other words, genera are aggregates of closely related species. For example, potato and brinjal are two different species but both belong to the genus Solanum.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following features can be said to be a true defining feature of living beings without any exception?
options: They can respond to external stimuli.
questions.solution: The most obvious and technically complicated feature of all living organisms is their ability to sense their surroundings or environment and respond to these environmental stimuli which could be physical, chemical or biological. All organisms, from the prokaryotes to the most complex eukaryotes can sense and respond to environmental cues eg. Photoperiod affects reproduction in seasonal breeders, both plants and animals. Consciousness therefore, becomes the defining property of living organisms. There are many organisms which do not reproduce e.g. mule. Thus, reproduction cannot be an all-inclusive defining characteristic of living organisms. However no non-living object is capable of reproducing or replicating by itself.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : In plant tissue culture, somatic embryos can be induced from any plant cell.
Reason : Any viable plant cell can differentiate into somatic embryos.
options: If both Assertion and Reason are true and the reason is the correct explanation of the assertion
If both Assertion and Reason are true but the reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion
If Assertion is true statement but Reason is false
if both Assertion and Reason are false statements
questions.solution: Any viable somatic cell of a plant has the ability to form full fledged plants. This ability of plant cell is known as cellular totipotency.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Select the correct sequence for Homo erectus.
questions.solution:
Taxonomical hierarchy shows arrangement in ascending order.
Species $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Division $\rightarrow$ Kingdom

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Assertion : Complexity of classification increases from kingdom to species.Reason : Common characters increase from kingdom to species.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false
questions.solution:
Classification involves hierarchy of steps, in which each step represents a rank or category, called taxonomic category or taxon. All taxa together constitute a taxonomic hierarchy as follows:
Species $\rightarrow$ Genus $\rightarrow$ Family $\rightarrow$ Order $\rightarrow$ Class $\rightarrow$ Phylum or division $\rightarrow$ Kingdom.
Most common characters among individual members are found in taxon species. Common characters decrease from species to kingdom and members of a kingdom have least number of common characters. Similarly, complexity of classification decreases from species to kingdom

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following is the correct scientific name of wheat derived by binominal nomenclature?
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion: Consciousness is considered as the defining property of living organisms.
Reason : All organisms, from the prokaryotes to the most complex eukaryotes can sense and respond to environmental stimuli.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Consciousness is awareness of the surroundings and responding to external stimuli. The external stimuli can be physical, chemical or biological. All organisms, from primitive prokaryotes to most advanced and complex eukaryotes, are able to sense and respond to environmental factors. Consciousness is thus said to be the defining property of living organisms.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Systematics is the branch of biology that deals with classification of living organism.
Reason : The aim of classification is to group the organisms in an orderly manner.
options: If both the assertion and reason are true and reason is a correct explanation of the assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but assertion is not a correct explanation of the assertion.
If the assertion is true but the reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Taxonomy or systematics is the branch of biology that deals with the classification of biological world of living organisms. The classification is the grouping of organisms in an orderly manner. This grouping is based upon similarities and dissimilarities.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Film forming chemical should not be used for checking transpiration.
Reason: Film forming chemicals interrupt transpiration, photosynthesis and respiration.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Film forming chemicals check transpiration by forming a thin film on the transpiring surface. They are sufficiently permeable to carbon dioxide and oxygen to allow photosynthesis and respiration but prevent movement of water vapours through them. These such chemicals can be used as antitranspirants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Translocation of sugar from source (leaf) to sink is defined as pressure flow hypothesis.
Reason: Translocation of the solute is facilitated through living phloem sieve tube cells.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: The accepted mechanism for the translocation of sugars from source to sink is called pressure flow hypothesis. The glucose is prepared at the source by photosynthesis, it is converted to sucrose (a disaccharide). The sugar is then moved in the form of sucrose into the companion cells and then into living phloem, sieve tube cells by active transport.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A plasmolysed cell can be deplasmolysed by placing it in
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A cell is equally permeable to sucrose solution and $\mathrm{NaCl}$ solution. First the cell is put in 0.6 $\mathrm{M}$ sucrose solution there is no change in size but when put in $0.6 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution the size will
questions.solution: $0.6 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution is hypertonic to $0.6 \mathrm{M}$ sucrose solution because there are more solutes per unit volume in $\mathrm{NaCl}$ solution as compared to sucrose solution. Hence, when the cell is placed in $0.6 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NaCl}$ solution after being placed in $0.6 \mathrm{M}$ sucrose solution water will move out of it and its size will decrease on account of exosmosis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Transpiration occurs only when stomata are open.
Reason : Transpiration occurs only through stomata.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Transpiration occurs through stomata, cuticles and lenticels. Stomata usually open during the day time and close during night. In succulents or CAM plants, the stomata remain closed during the day and open only during the night. As cuticle, lenticel and bark lack any mechanism of closure, transpiration occurs throughout day and night.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The concentration of solute in four cells is $0.4 \mathrm{M}$. They are placed in four separate containers I, II, III and IV, filled with saline water of concentrations $0.1 \mathrm{M}, 0.4 \mathrm{M}, 2 \mathrm{M}$ and $3 \mathrm{M}$ respectively. In which container will the cell swell?
questions.solution: When cell having solute concentration of $0.4 \mathrm{M}$ is placed in a saline water of concentration $0.1 \mathrm{M}$, the outer medium, i.e., the saline water is hypotonic to the cell. Hence, the water will move inside the cell (endosmosis) and as a result the cell will swell up.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: $\mathrm{Na}^{+} / \mathrm{K}^{+}$pump is associated with
questions.solution: Sodium-potassium pump $\left(\mathrm{Na}^{+}-\mathrm{K}^{+}\right)$is a membrane transport protein that exchanges sodium ions $\left(\mathrm{Na}^{+}\right)$for potassium ions $\left(\mathrm{K}^{+}\right)$. It transports $3 \mathrm{Na}^{+}$outwards for $2 \mathrm{~K}^{+}$into the cell, thus maintaining the differential concentrations of each ion across the plasma membrane. The process requires energy in the form of ATP, being a form of active transport.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Identify (i), (ii) and (iii) in the given diagram.
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In active transport, carrier proteins are used, which use energy in the form of ATP, to
options: transport molecules against concentration gradient of cell wall.
transport molecules along concentration gradient of cell membrane.
transport molecules against concentration gradient of cell membrane.
transport molecules along concentration gradient of cell wall.
questions.solution: In active transport, carrier proteins which are located at membrane, use energy in the form of ATP to transport molecules ag ainst concentration gradient of cell membrane. Transportation rate reaches maximum when all carrier proteins are being used or are saturated. These proteins are very specific to certain substances and are sensitive to inhibitors.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Water that enters into a plant cell through diffusion makes it turgid.
Reason : Entry of water into the cell through diffusion develops wall pressure inside the cell.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Cell's turgidity is due to turgor pressure. Turgor pressure is the pressure which develops in the confined part of an osmotic system due to osmotic entry of water into it. It is also called hydrostatic pressure or pressure potential. The force exerted by the cell wall over the protoplast is called wall pressure. Normally wall pressure is equal and opposite to turgor pressure except when the cell becomes flaccid.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What do $A, B, C$ and $D$ represent in the following figure?
options: $\mathrm{A}$ : carrier protein, $\mathrm{B}$ : symport, C: uniport, D : antiport
A : carrier protein, B : uniport, C: antiport, D : symport
A : carrier protein, B : antiport, C: symport, D : uniport
A : carrier protein, B : uniport, C: symport, $\mathrm{D}$ : antiport
questions.solution: No Solution Available

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Shrinkage of the protoplast of a cell occurs under the influence of hypertonic solution.
Reason : Hypertonic solution causes plasmolysis.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Shrinkage of the protoplast of a cell (due to water loss) under the influence of a hypertonic solution is called plasmolysis. Hypertonic solution causes exosmosis or withdrawal of water from cytoplasm and then the central vacuole of cell. The size of cytoplasm as well as central vacuole and hence protoplast becomes reduced. The pressure on the wall is simultaneously reduced and the elastic wall contracts causing a reduction in cell size.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Movement of materials inside phloem is bidirectional i.e. it can be both upwards or downwards.
Reason : Movement of molecules inside xylem is unidirectional i.e. always upwards.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Long distance bulk movement of substances that occurs through conducting or vascular tissues of plants is called translocation. There are two vascular tissues, xylem and phloem. Xylem translocation is mainly from roots to aerial parts. It passes water with mineral salts, some organic nitrogen and hormones i.e. unidirectional. Phloem translocates organic substances and inorganic solutes first from leaves to all other parts of the plant and storage organs. Storage organs re-export organic nutrients to those parts which require the same as newly formed leaves and fruits i.e it is bidirectional.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which of the following helps in ascent of sap?
questions.solution: Water is mainly absorbed by roots which goes upwards so as to replace water loss in transpiration and to be used in photosynthesis. This upward movement of water from roots to leaves through stem against force of gravity is called ascent of sap. Different theories have been put forward in support of ascent of sap. They include root pressure theory, capillary theory and transpiration pull theory. Root pressure is developed when rate of absorption is more than rate of transpiration and so water is pushed up in the tracheary elements. Capillary force theory was given by Bohm, according to which xylem vessels act as minute capillaries and water rises in these capillaries due to capillary action or surface tension. According to transpiration pull theory by Dixon and Jolly, due to water loss by transpiration suction pressure is increased and water is absorbed from adjacent xylem vessels of leaves and thus sap in under tension.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In succulent plants the stomata open in night and close in day. Which among the following would be best hypothesis to explain the mechanism of stomatal action in night only?
options: \(\mathrm{CO}_2\) accumulates, reduces \(\mathrm{pH}\), stimulate enzymes resulting in accumulation of sugars.
Increase in \(\mathrm{CO}_2\) concentration, conversion of organic acids into starch resulting in the increased conversion into sugars resulting in \(\mathrm{K}^{+}\)transport.
Low \(\mathrm{CO}_2\) concentration accumulates organic acids resulting in the increased concentration of cell sap.
\(\mathrm{CO}_2\) used up, increase \(\mathrm{pH}\) results in accumulation of sugars.
questions.solution:
In CAM plants stomata open at night. In these plants night acidification occurs. i.e., malic acid is synthesised during night due to incomplete oxidation of carbohydrates.
In night, $2 \mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_6+3 \mathrm{O}_2 \rightarrow 3 \mathrm{C}_4 \mathrm{H}_6 \mathrm{O}_5+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (malic acid)
$\downarrow$ leads to opening of stomata At night malic acid formed in guard cells dissociates into $\mathrm{H}^{+}$and malate ions. $\mathrm{K}^{+}$ion exchange from subsidiary cells with $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ions. Thus due to accumulation of $\mathrm{K}^{+}$ions osmotic pressure of guard cells increases, endosmosis occurs anDguard cells become turgid due to which stomata opens. The malic acid at day time breaks into $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and pyruvic acid thus increasing the concentration of $\mathrm{CO}_2$ in mesophyll cells. This is utilised in Calvin cycle to form sugar which is finally converted into starch.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: What is correct about the movement of substance across the membrane in facilitated diffusion?
options: It doesn't cause transport of molecules from low concentration to high concentration.
It is a very specific transport.
questions.solution: In facilitated diffusion special proteins help move substances across membranes along the concentration gradient without expenditure of energy. Facilitated diffusion is very specific, it allows cell to select substances for uptake. It is sensitive to inhibitors which react with protein side chains.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Specific proteins responsible for the flow of materials and information into the cell are called
questions.solution: Active transport is the uphill movement of materials across the membrane where the solute particles move against their concentration gradient or electro-chemical gradient. It takes place through the agency of special organic molecules called carrier molecules, carrier particles or carrier proteins. There is a special carrier molecule for each solute particle (ion or molecule). The carrier has its binding site on two surfaces of the membrane. The solute particle (or substrate) combines with the carrier to form carrier solute complex. In the bound state the carrier undergoes a conformational change which transports the solute to the other side of the membrane. Here the solute is released. Energy is used in bringing about the conformational change in the carrier. It is provided by ATP.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The sugarcane plant has
questions.solution: Guard cells are semilunar or kidney shaped cells which surrounds the minute apertures of epidermis called stomata. Guard cells are living and have chloroplast. In sugarcane and in some other monocots like doob, maize guard cells are dumb bell shaped.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : When the ambient temperature is high and soil contains excess of water, the plants tend to lose water in the form of droplets from lenticels.
Reason : Root pressure regulates the rate of loss of water form lenticels.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of the assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of the assertion
If assertion is true, but reason is false
Both assertion and reason are false statements.
questions.solution: When the ambient temperature is high and soil contains excess water, the rate of transpiration is increased i.e., loss of water takes place from leaves through stomata. Lenticular transpiration is only $0.1 \%$ of the total transpiration. It however continues day and night because lenticels have no mechanism of closure. The lenticels connect the atmospheric air with the cortical tissue of stem through intercellular spaces present amongst the complementary cells. Root pressure regulates the rate of loss of water from the stomata in some plants. High temperature may cause desiccation and closure of stomata. Root pressure is a positive pressure that develops in the xylem sap of the roots of some plants.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Wooden doors swell up and get stuck during the rainy season. This is due to the phenomenon of
questions.solution: Jamming of wooden frames during rains is caused by swelling of wood due to imbibition. (The absorption of water by the solid particles of a substance without forming a solution is called imbibition).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Stomata opens
options: in day time - for gaseous exchange
in day time - no gaseous exchange
in night time only - for gaseous exchange
in night time only - no gaseous exchange.
questions.solution: Stomata are meant for gaseous exchange but are also the main source of transpiration. In majority of plants stomata open in the presence of light i.e., in daytime and close in darkness (night).

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : A plant girdled upto bast will die. 
Reason : Food transportation in such plant will cease followed by ceasation of water transport.
options: If both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is the correct explantion of Assertion
If both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is not the correct explanation of Assertion
If Assertion is true but Reason is false
If Assertion is false but Reason is true.
questions.solution: A plant girdled upto bast will show below the girdled part, the non transportation of the food which occurs through phloem (bast). Water transport from roots to other plant parts via xylem will continue till root cells do not die due to starvation.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: Which one of the following statements is correct with respect to salt water balance inside the body of living organisms?
options: When water is not available camels do not produce urine but store urea in tissues.
Salmon fish excretes lot of stored salt through gill membrane when in fresh water.
Paramecium discharges concentrated salt solution by contractile vacuoles.
The body fluids of fresh water animals are generally hypotonic to surrounding water.
questions.solution: The dwelling place of camels is desert where there is scarcity of water, camel excrete a highly concentrated urine to conserve water. They do not sweat till body temperature rises about $55^{\circ} \mathrm{C}-60^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Camels can also tolerate desiccation upto $40 \%$ cellular content.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: The water logging capacity of garden soil will be determined by measuring the weight of
options: soil by adding water and humus
water saturated soil then heating the soil till it is dry and then measuring the weight of soil
soil by adding water, biomass and humus
soil from different locations with biomass.
questions.solution: The optimum or maximum amount of water retained per unit dry weight of soil after the stoppage of gravitational flow is called field capacity. Soil moisture beyond field capacity produces water logging. The soil with high water retention and low or negligible percolation rate is prone to water logging.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In a $50 \mathrm{gm}$ living tissue, what would be the amount of water?
questions.solution: Water is the most abundant chemical compound in living organism. In a cell, water occupies $70-90 \%$ of the cellular mass. So, in 50 $\mathrm{gm}$ of living tissue 35-45 gm of water would be present.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q:
Assertion : Light is one of the important factor in transpiration.
Reason : It induces stomatal opening and closing. Therefore, transpiration increases in light and decreases in dark.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion
If assertion is true but reason is false
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Light has got no direct effect on the rate of transpiration but indirectly it affects the rate in two ways firstly by controlling the stomatal opening and secondly by affecting the temperature. With the increase in the light intensity the rate of transpiration increases because the stomata get opened and the temperature increases. The rate of transpiration increases markedly in light and decreases in dark. There is a close relationship between the opening of stomata and presence of light.one of the raw materials for photosynthesis, its concentration affects the rate of photosynthesis markedly. Because of its very low concentration in atmosphere $(0.03$ per cent only) it acts as a limiting factor in natural photosynthesis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: If turgor pressure becomes equal to the wall pressure, then
options: no exchange of water takes place
solute goes from the cell into water.
questions.solution: The pressure that develops in a cell from time to time due to osmotic diffusion of water molecules into a cell that causes stretching of the cell wall is called turgor pressure. Due to turgor pressure, rigid cell wall offers resistance. This resistance works in a direction opposite to turgor pressure. But it is equal to turgor pressure and is called wall pressure. In this condition, no exchange of water takes place.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: A human RBC is placed in $1.5 \%$ salt solution. It will
questions.solution: For human $\mathrm{RBC}, 0.9 \%$ salt solution is isotonic. Hence a medium of $1.5 \%$ salt solution would be hypertonic i.e. more concentrated. Hence, the RBC will shrink due to exosmosis.

questions.subject: Botany
questions.q: In plants, water moves from
options: less negative to more negative potential
more negative to less negative potential
questions.solution: Water always moves from the area of high water potential to the area of low water potential i.e., from less negative potential to more negative potential.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Among the following, the number of compounds having zero lone pair of electrons on central atom is
$\mathrm{XeO}_3, \mathrm{XeO}_2 \mathrm{~F}_2, \mathrm{XeO}_4, \mathrm{XeO}_3 \mathrm{~F}_2, \mathrm{XeF}_4$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{XeO}_4$ and $\mathrm{XeO}_3 \mathrm{~F}_2$ do not have lone pair of electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Assertion : $\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$ and $\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$ both exist.
Reason : Both have same valence electrons.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Both having same structure and same valance electrons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which is least soluble in water?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{MgS}$ is most covalent so it is least soluble in water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Assertion : $\mathrm{LiCl}$ is predominantly a covalent compound.
Reason : Electronegativity difference between $\mathrm{Li}$ and $\mathrm{Cl}$ is too small.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Electronegativity difference between $\mathrm{Li}$ and $\mathrm{Cl}$ is quite large. Covalent compound is formed due to high ionisation energy of Li or small size and greater polarising power of $\mathrm{Li}$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which pair of molecules does not have identical structure?
questions.solution: $\mathrm{BF}_3 \rightarrow$ Trigonal planar $\mathrm{ICl}_3 \rightarrow \mathrm{T}$-shape

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: When $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$reacts with excess of sodium thiosulphate then the obtained species have charge and geometry respectively
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Ag}^{+}+2 \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3^{2-} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{Ag}\left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{O}_3\right)_2\right]^{3-}$
Coordination number of $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}$is 2 so it has $s p$-hybridization and has linear shape.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Select the correct statement regarding shapes of $\mathrm{PCl}_5, \mathrm{BrF}_5$ and $\mathrm{IF}_7$.
options: One of the given compounds is square pyramidal.
One of the given compounds is tetrahedral.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (c) : } \mathrm{PCl}_5: \\ & b p=5 ; l p=0 \\ & \text { Total }=5 \\ & s p^3 d \\ & \text { (Trigonal bipyramidal) } \\ & \mathrm{BrF}_5: \\ & b p=5 ; l p=1 \\ & \text { Total }=6, s p^3 d^2 \\ & \text { (Square pyramidal) }\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{IF}_7$ : $\begin{aligned} & b p=7 ; l p=0 \\ & \text { Total }=7 \\ & s p^3 d^3\end{aligned}$ (Pentagonal bipyramidal)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Assertion : $\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{Si}$ bonds are much stronger than $\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds.
Reason : Silicon forms double bonds with itself.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{Si}$ bonds are weaker than $\mathrm{Si}-\mathrm{O}$ bonds. Si has no tendency to form double bonds with itself.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q: Which of the following have same hybridisation but are not isostructural?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Which of the following is correct regarding bond angles?
(i) $\mathrm{SO}_2 < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
(ii) $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S} < \mathrm{SO}_2$
(iii) $\mathrm{SO}_2 < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S}$
(iv) $\mathrm{SbH}_3 < \mathrm{NO}_2^{+}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{lllll}\mathrm{SbH}_3 & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} & \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~S} & \mathrm{SO}_2 & \mathrm{NO}_2^{+}\end{array}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.q:
Assertion: The second dissociation constant of fumaric acid is greater than maleic acid.
Reason : Higher the dissociation constant of acid, more is the acidic character.
options: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.
questions.solution: Both fumaric and maleic acids have two ionisable $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ions i.e., protons. The maleate monoanion shows intramolecular $\mathrm{H}$-bonding, whereas fumarate monoanion shows intermolecular $\mathrm{H}$-bonding. Thus, fumarate monoanion requires more energy to give fumarate dianion. Hence, second dissociation of fumaric acid is more than that of maleic acid.
